frameworkNotes: Reports voluntarily after Omnibus removed bunq from CSRD scope. Self-described approach: 'transparency shouldn't depend on regulatory obligation.' Methodology cites PCAF for financed emissions; no GHG Protocol Scope 2 location/market split.

assurance.scopeNote: No external assurance in 2024. bunq states they 'will revisit assurance options as the regulatory landscape evolves.'

scope1.methodNote: Direct emissions from fuel combustion in office heating systems. 2024 expanded methodology to include all global locations using FTE-based approach anchored in Amsterdam. Per FTE: 0.6.

scope2.methodNote: Indirect emissions from purchased electricity, heat, cooling. Per FTE: 1.7. Note: bunq does NOT split location-based vs market-based — sub-GHG-Protocol-standard disclosure.

scope3.factorSource: Self-calculated; partner emission factors from card suppliers, AWS (claims 100% renewable match), DHL GoGreen Plus
scope3.note: Cards production 25.3, Cards distribution 0.03, Cloud computing 0.455, DHL 1.3. Card production is the largest line; full transition to AWS reduced cloud emissions.
Excluded as immaterial per bunq inventory.
E-waste 0.0, Event waste 17.0, Customer-related waste 1.1. Event waste tracked systematically for the first time in 2024.

scope3.factorSource: Travel agency activity data; emission factors not disclosed
scope3.note: Stable YoY (160 in 2023). Internal inconsistency in source: text body lists '166.1' on p.6 vs '166.4' in p.5 table. Commitment: increase rail, minimize air travel.
Down from 138 in 2023 thanks to flexible 2-day-WFH policy. 2024 data-quality enhancement initiative improved figures.
Excluded as immaterial. Note: customer-spending emissions (the bunq Business spend Carbo monitors) would naturally fit here — bunq does not currently disclose them.

CFG lending: AF 134,937 + ABL 418 + CRE 22,332 = 157,687 tCO2. Own investments: DRM 33,757 + RBS 423 + SCB 373 + Swap collateral 977 + ABS 53,839 + CLO 60,117 + Bonds 1,815 = 151,301 tCO2. The CLO line carries 275 tCO2/€M intensity (highest in portfolio).

risks.description: Financing carbon-intensive assets (especially Asset Finance with 463 tCO2/€M and CLO portfolio at 275 tCO2/€M) creates exposure to transition-policy risk as EU tightens carbon pricing.
Cloud-services energy demand grows with bunq's user base; while AWS claims 100% renewable matching, the underlying grid intensity remains a passthrough risk.

climateCommittees: ESG Team (quarterly updates to both boards)

methodology.ghgProtocolVersion: Implicit (not formally cited); uses PCAF for Scope 3 Cat 15
factorSources: PCAF (Partnership for Carbon Accounting Financials) — financed emissions
Self-calculated for Scope 1 + 2 (no public factor library cited)
methodology.materialityMethod: Single materiality (financial / risk-based). Likelihood × Impact thresholds defined but no stakeholder consultation log disclosed.

actionSummary: Veritree partnership: 13M+ mangroves planted since 2019 (Eden Reforestation Projects), 314,000 tCO2 claimed sequestration in 2024, 15.9M trees ordered total, 4.3M+ planted via Veritree, Kilifi region 2.7M of 3.4M ordered, 1,592 ha under restoration, 31,848 workdays. AWS migration completed (cloud emissions ~0.46 tCO2). Card production switched to more sustainable suppliers (25.3 tCO2 vs 77.5 in 2023). DHL GoGreen Plus for shipping. Sustainable Banking Coalition member. ABL portfolio scaled back from €39M to €11M to phase out less sustainable lending.

warnings: Scope 1+2 total 119.8 vs location-table 83 unreconciled gap
Scope 2 not split location vs market — below GHG Protocol standard
No targets, no transition plan, no SBTi, no net-zero year, no double materiality
Cat 11 'use of sold products' marked immaterial — but customer-spending emissions arguably belong here for a bank
